questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The area of the region bounded between the curves $y=e\|x|\ln | x\|, x^2+y^2-2(|x|+|y|)+1 \geq 0$ and $x$-axis where $|x| \leq 1$, if $\alpha$ is the $x$-coordinate of the point of intersection of curves in $1^{\text {st }}$ quadrant, is
options: $4\left[\int_0^\alpha e x \ln x d x-\int_1^\alpha\left(1-\sqrt{1-(x-1)^2}\right) d x\right]$
questions.solution: Required area is $2\left[\int_0^a e x \ln x+\int_1^a\left(1-\sqrt{1-(x-1)^2}\right) d x\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The area (in square units) bounded by the curves $y=\sqrt{x}, 2 y-x+3=0, x$-axis and lying in the first quadrant is:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Area under } y \text { Axis }=\left|\int_{y_1}^{y_2} x d y\right| \\ & \text { Area }=\int_0^3\left(2 y+3-y^2\right) d y \\ & =9\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The area between the curve $y^2(a+x)=(a-x)^3$ and its vertical asymptote is
questions.solution: $x=-a$ is the only asymptote to the given curve $\mathrm{A}=2 \int_{-a}^{\mathrm{a}} \mathrm{ydx}=3 \pi \mathrm{a}^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The area of the region bounded by the curve $C: y=\frac{x+1}{x^2+1}$ and the line $L: y=1$, is
questions.solution:
On solving $C: y=\frac{x+1}{x^2+1}$ and the line $L: y=1$, we have
$\frac{x+1}{x^2+1}=1 \Rightarrow x+1=x^2+1 \Rightarrow x(x-1)=0 \Rightarrow x=0,1$ Thus, the points of intersection are at x = 0 and x = 1. Required area $=\int_0^1\left(\frac{x+1}{x^2+1}-1\right) d x=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^1 \frac{2 x}{x^2+1} d x+\int_0^1 \frac{1}{x^2+1} d x-\int_0^1 d x$ $=\left[\frac{1}{2} \ln \left(\mathrm{x}^2+1\right)+\left(\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{x}\right)-\mathrm{x}\right]_0^1=\frac{1}{2} \ln 2+\frac{\pi}{4}-1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the tangent to the curve $y=1-x^2$ at $x=\alpha(0 \lt \alpha \lt 1)$ meets the axes at $P$ and $Q$. Also $\alpha$ varies, the minimum value of the area of the triangle OPQ is $k$ times the area bounded by the axes and the part of the curve for which $0 \lt x \lt 1$, then $k$ is
questions.solution:
Area under curve and axis $=\int_0^1\left(1-\mathrm{x}^2\right) \mathrm{dx}=\frac{2}{3}$ at point $\left(\alpha, 1-\alpha^2\right)$ tangent is
$\left[y-\left(1-\alpha^2\right)\right]=-2 \alpha(x-\alpha)$
$\Rightarrow 2 \alpha x+y=\alpha^2+1 \Rightarrow P \equiv\left(\frac{\alpha^2+1}{2 \alpha}, 0\right), Q \equiv\left(0, \alpha^2+1\right)$
Hence, $Q \equiv\left(2,1+4 x_1-x_1^2\right)$
$\therefore A=\left[\left(1+2 x_1-x_1^2\right)+\left(1+4 x_1-x_1^2\right)\right]=1-3 x_1-x_1^2$
$\frac{\mathrm{dA}}{\mathrm{dx}}=-3-2 \mathrm{x}_1 \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}_1=-\frac{3}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
